questions.q: Hyder Ali became the de facto ruler of which Indian kingdom?
questions.explanation: Hyder Ali rose to become the de facto ruler of the Kingdom of Mysore.

questions.q: In which century did Hyder Ali rise to power?
questions.explanation: Hyder Ali rose to power in Mysore during the later 18th century CE.

questions.q: Hyder Ali is remembered for pioneering the military use of what weapon?
questions.explanation: Hyder Ali employed rockets in warfare, a technology further developed by his son Tipu Sultan.

questions.q: Hyder Ali fought wars against which European power in India?
questions.explanation: Hyder Ali fought the British in the first two Anglo-Mysore Wars.

questions.q: Which famous ruler of Mysore was the son of Hyder Ali?
questions.explanation: Tipu Sultan, the 'Tiger of Mysore', was the son and successor of Hyder Ali.

questions.q: Hyder Ali began his career primarily as a what?
questions.explanation: Hyder Ali began as a soldier and rose through the ranks of the Mysore army by his military skill.

questions.q: The Anglo-Mysore Wars were fought in which part of India?
questions.explanation: The Anglo-Mysore Wars were fought in southern India, where Mysore was located.

questions.q: What did Hyder Ali build to make Mysore a formidable military power?
questions.explanation: Hyder Ali built a strong, modern army that made Mysore a major military power in the south.

questions.q: Which dynasty did Hyder Ali effectively sideline while ruling Mysore in their name?
questions.explanation: Hyder Ali ruled as de facto sovereign while the Wodeyar kings of Mysore became figureheads.

questions.q: Hyder Ali's capital was the same fortified island city used by Tipu Sultan. What was it?
questions.explanation: Hyder Ali made Srirangapatna (Seringapatam) the seat of Mysore power, later defended by Tipu Sultan.

questions.q: The First Anglo-Mysore War, fought by Hyder Ali, ended in roughly which year?
questions.explanation: The First Anglo-Mysore War ended in 1769 with the Treaty of Madras.

questions.q: The First Anglo-Mysore War concluded with which treaty, favourable to Hyder Ali?
questions.explanation: The First Anglo-Mysore War ended with the Treaty of Madras in 1769.

questions.q: During the First Anglo-Mysore War, Hyder Ali boldly threatened which British settlement directly?
questions.explanation: Hyder Ali advanced to the gates of Madras in 1769, forcing the British to negotiate the Treaty of Madras.

questions.q: In which year did Hyder Ali die during the Second Anglo-Mysore War?
questions.explanation: Hyder Ali died in 1782 while the Second Anglo-Mysore War was still being fought; Tipu Sultan continued it.

questions.q: Hyder Ali's two main rivals besides the British in the Deccan included the Marathas and which other power?
questions.explanation: Hyder Ali contended with the Marathas and the Nizam of Hyderabad as well as the British.

questions.q: In the Second Anglo-Mysore War, Hyder Ali allied with the French against which common enemy?
questions.explanation: Hyder Ali allied with the French against the British during the Second Anglo-Mysore War.

questions.q: Hyder Ali rose to prominence under the Wodeyar kingdom by serving in what capacity before seizing power?
questions.explanation: Hyder Ali gained control as a leading military commander before becoming the kingdom's effective ruler around 1761.

questions.q: Hyder Ali took effective control of Mysore in approximately which year?
questions.explanation: Hyder Ali became the de facto ruler of Mysore around 1761.

questions.q: Hyder Ali's rockets were notable for being constructed with what feature that boosted their range?
questions.explanation: Mysorean rockets used iron tubes to hold the propellant, giving greater thrust and range than earlier designs.

questions.q: Hyder Ali was known for being illiterate yet what?
options: A highly capable administrator and general
questions.explanation: Though largely unlettered, Hyder Ali was a formidable general and effective administrator of Mysore.

questions.q: Hyder Ali expanded Mysore at the expense of neighbouring states including the Nayaks of which region on the coast?
questions.explanation: Hyder Ali extended Mysore's control into the Malabar coast and other neighbouring territories.

questions.q: Which British commander did Hyder Ali's forces confront repeatedly in the Carnatic during the Second Anglo-Mysore War?
questions.explanation: Sir Eyre Coote led British forces against Hyder Ali in battles such as Porto Novo during the Second Anglo-Mysore War.

questions.q: Hyder Ali inflicted a notable defeat on a British force at which 1780 battle in the Carnatic?
questions.explanation: At the Battle of Pollilur (1780), Hyder Ali's forces, using rockets, badly defeated a British detachment under Colonel Baillie.

questions.q: Hyder Ali's birthplace is generally given as which town in present-day Karnataka?
questions.explanation: Hyder Ali is traditionally said to have been born at Budikote in present-day Karnataka around 1720.

questions.q: The Wodeyar monarch whom Hyder Ali reduced to a figurehead while consolidating power was which king?
questions.explanation: Hyder Ali sidelined Krishnaraja Wodeyar II, ruling Mysore in the Wodeyar name while holding real power.

questions.q: Hyder Ali died in 1782 of an illness, commonly recorded as what?
questions.explanation: Hyder Ali died in December 1782 of a malignant growth (often described as a carbuncle or tumour) during the war.

questions.q: Hyder Ali allied with the Marathas and the Nizam against the British, but this coalition was undermined when which partner made a separate peace?
options: The Marathas and the Nizam both defected to the British
questions.explanation: The triple alliance broke down as the Marathas and the Nizam came to terms with the British, leaving Mysore to fight largely alone.

questions.q: Hyder Ali's mausoleum, the Gumbaz, where he was later interred alongside Tipu, is located at which site?
questions.explanation: Hyder Ali and Tipu Sultan are buried in the Gumbaz mausoleum at Srirangapatna.

questions.q: Hyder Ali's modernisation of Mysore's military relied significantly on training and arms support from which European nation?
questions.explanation: The French supplied military expertise and arms to Hyder Ali, aligning with him against the British.

questions.q: The Second Anglo-Mysore War, begun under Hyder Ali, was concluded after his death by which 1784 treaty?
questions.explanation: The Second Anglo-Mysore War ended with the Treaty of Mangalore in 1784, signed by Tipu Sultan after Hyder Ali's death.
